shootDirection: the group at the front of a stage during soundcheck, lit warmly from the side, shot close from the empty floor looking up at them
concept: Front-of-stage soundcheck portrait from the empty floor
scene: At the very front edge of a small theatre stage during late-afternoon soundcheck, four chosen figures cluster naturally in a tight, upward-looking composition from the empty floor: closest to camera, the woman from the first reference leans on one hand at the lip of the stage, her braids, cross earring and dark lipstick catching a warm amber sidelight; just behind her, the long-haired man in the black turtleneck from the second reference turns slightly toward her with a calm half-smile, while deeper in frame the tall tattooed man from the first reference stands half in haze near a mic stand, head lifted into the light; anchoring the opposite side, the moustached man in the wide-brim hat from the second reference crouches near a monitor wedge, red scarf vivid against dark clothing as he glances off toward the wings. The whole image feels like one real paused moment between songs, with stage smoke, coiled cables, lacquered floor reflections and glowing side light making every face large, sharp and luminous in saturated editorial colour.
prompt: Create one original, photorealistic 16:9 group photograph that brings together the musicians shown across the 2 reference images into a single shared real-world scene. Scene: At the very front edge of a small theatre stage during late-afternoon soundcheck, four chosen figures cluster naturally in a tight, upward-looking composition from the empty floor: closest to camera, the woman from the first reference leans on one hand at the lip of the stage, her braids, cross earring and dark lipstick catching a warm amber sidelight; just behind her, the long-haired man in the black turtleneck from the second reference turns slightly toward her with a calm half-smile, while deeper in frame the tall tattooed man from the first reference stands half in haze near a mic stand, head lifted into the light; anchoring the opposite side, the moustached man in the wide-brim hat from the second reference crouches near a monitor wedge, red scarf vivid against dark clothing as he glances off toward the wings. The whole image feels like one real paused moment between songs, with stage smoke, coiled cables, lacquered floor reflections and glowing side light making every face large, sharp and luminous in saturated editorial colour. Keep every person clearly recognisable and faithful to their reference photo - preserve each face, skin tone, hair, facial hair, glasses, and overall likeness exactly as shown. Do not invent anyone who is not in the reference photos. Keep this an intimate, well-composed group, not a crowd. Where a reference photo shows a band, feature only its single most recognisable member rather than every member. Frame the group tightly - close or medium-close - so every face is large in the frame, sharp, and richly detailed, never small or distant. Arrange them naturally within the scene as one connected group sharing the same moment and light, like a real posed or candid group photo. Vary posture, depth, and where people are looking - this is a caught moment, not a band-publicity lineup evenly spaced and facing the camera. Not separate cut-outs floating side by side, and never a grid, row, or panel of squares. Render photorealistically: real skin texture, true-to-life lighting and depth of field, captured as if shot on a professional camera. This is a real photograph, not an illustration, drawing, painting, cartoon, or render. Colour treatment: bright, vivid, richly saturated editorial colour with luminous highlights and punchy contrast, drawing on amber, crimson, midnight black, electric cyan, smoky silver. Mood: intimate, magnetic, poised. Do not add any extra people beyond the reference subjects. Do not duplicate, merge, blend, or distort faces. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, illustration, illustrated, drawing, painting, cartoon, comic, anime, vector art, sketch, flat graphic style, extra people, background crowd, duplicated faces, cloned faces, merged faces, blended faces, morphed features, distorted faces, deformed hands, extra limbs. Photorealistic, sharp, high quality, flattering, with real depth and a strong sense of place.
